projects.title: God of War: Sons of Sparta

content.alt: God of War: Sons of Sparta Project Thumbnail

sectionMedia.content: Built a custom 2D lighting pipeline to fix a CPU bottleneck in Unity's default system, which choked under hundreds of lights. Gives artists 256 individually-lit sprite layers with negligible CPU cost on PS5 — even with hundreds of lights in a scene.

content: Lighting: Stencil-buffer bit encoding isolates each light to its sprite layer
Tooling: Built an in-editor tool to serve a team of 20+ lighting artists; enables authoring/placing lights with no code
Optimization: All lights/shadows instanced to one draw call per blend mode; GPU & CPU cost effectively flat regardless of light count

sectionMedia.content: Authored 13+ production-ready sprite shaders underpinning character/environment interaction with the lighting system, as well as procedural surface effects across both.

content: Shaders: Pixel-perfect running water, foliage, screen-space reflections, directional lights
Optimization: All sprite shaders use instancing or SRP Batcher to minimize batch & draw call count on PS5

sectionMedia.content: Led R&D on a scalable GPU-driven grass system for close-up-quality stadium turf with a nostalgic look. Renders 1M+ triangles in a single draw call at ~5ms render cost on Nintendo Switch.

content: Culling: A ComputeShader generates and culls instances using a Morton-ordered, groupshared-cached grid; async, double-buffered CPU <-> GPU transfer avoids pipeline stalls, profiled with Nvidia Nsight Graphics

Rendering: Custom low-overhead hook into Camera.Render drives per-camera culling; Shader Graph shader derives color/height from a world-space LUT instead of per-blade textures (3 lookups, ~128 tris per blade)
Tooling: Modeled/UV'd all grass in Blender; built an in-editor tool for artists to hand-paint per-stadium color/height LUTs

sectionMedia.content: Worked cross-department with Level Design to combine environment meshes, Materials, and textures across the project to fit within Nintendo Switch memory budgets — cutting batch count by 50% and memory usage by 75%.
